pr.title: fix(APP-1892): restore PostHog onboarding-completed and create-pr-button-clicked events
pr.body:
- [x] A human has tested these changes.

---

## Why

[APP-1892](https://linear.app/all-hands-ai/issue/APP-1892/bug-saas-posthog-events-no-longer-firing-in-saas) reports two PostHog events have stopped firing in SaaS:
- `onboarding_completed` — stopped reporting ~22 days ago
- `create_pr_button_clicked` — stopped firing ~9 days ago

Root cause: PR #14006 ("feat: move PostHog server side") deleted the client-side `posthog.capture(...)` calls but the server-side replacements were incomplete:
1. The new `submit_onboarding` handler in `enterprise/server/routes/onboarding.py` was created with the right analytics calls, but its `onboarding_router` was never registered in `enterprise/saas_server.py`. Meanwhile the frontend kept POSTing to `/api/complete_onboarding` (in `auth.py`), which only flipped a DB flag and never fired analytics.
2. No server-side replacement was added for `create_pr_button_clicked` anywhere — the button click only fills a chat prompt and never reaches the backend.

NPS surveys keyed off the PR button and onboarding analytics for group targeting are both blind as a result.

## Summary

- **`onboarding completed`**: consolidated the analytics call into the existing `POST /api/complete_onboarding` (which the frontend already calls). Accept a typed `OnboardingSubmission` body, fire `analytics.track_onboarding_completed(...)` and an org `group_identify(...)` to stamp `onboarding_completed_at`, all wrapped in try/except so analytics failures never block onboarding. Removed the orphan `enterprise/server/routes/onboarding.py` + its tests to leave a single source of truth.
- **`create pr button clicked`**: added `CREATE_PR_BUTTON_CLICKED` constant + typed `AnalyticsService.track_create_pr_button_clicked(...)`. New enterprise-only `POST /api/analytics/events/create-pr-button-clicked` endpoint (registered in `saas_server.py`) with a `Literal`-constrained `git_provider` payload so arbitrary client strings can't become PostHog properties. Frontend mutation hook fires from both `GitControlBarPrButton` and `GitToolsSubmenu` (the chat button has a sibling in the tools submenu); errors are deliberately swallowed so analytics never blocks the click.
- Tests covering: happy path, unauthenticated request, analytics disabled, analytics-exception isolation, `group_identify` skipped when no org, payload validation rejecting unknown providers.

## Issue Number

APP-1892

## How to Test

Backend unit tests (everything I touched plus regression checks):
```bash
# OSS analytics service (58 tests, includes the 2 new ones for track_create_pr_button_clicked)
poetry run pytest tests/unit/test_analytics_service.py

# Enterprise: new analytics events router (6 tests)
PYTHONPATH=".:$PYTHONPATH" poetry run --project=enterprise pytest \
  enterprise/tests/unit/server/routes/test_analytics_events.py -v \
  -p no:ddtrace -p no:ddtrace.pytest_bdd -p no:ddtrace.pytest_benchmark

# Enterprise: onboarding endpoint (21 tests, includes 4 new analytics tests)
PYTHONPATH=".:$PYTHONPATH" poetry run --project=enterprise pytest \
  enterprise/tests/unit/server/routes/test_auth_onboarding.py -v \
  -p no:ddtrace -p no:ddtrace.pytest_bdd -p no:ddtrace.pytest_benchmark

# Broader auth-routes regression (51 tests should all still pass)
PYTHONPATH=".:$PYTHONPATH" poetry run --project=enterprise pytest \
  enterprise/tests/unit/test_auth_routes.py \
  -p no:ddtrace -p no:ddtrace.pytest_bdd -p no:ddtrace.pytest_benchmark
```

Frontend:
```bash
cd frontend
npm run test -- --run __tests__/hooks/mutation/use-track-create-pr-button-clicked.test.tsx
npm run lint:fix   # 0 errors (5 pre-existing warnings in unrelated files)
npm run build      # clean
```

Route registration sanity check (verifies both routes appear on the SaaS app):
```bash
FRONTEND_DIRECTORY=/tmp/fake-frontend POSTHOG_CLIENT_KEY=dummy \
  PYTHONPATH=".:$PYTHONPATH" poetry run --project=enterprise python -c "
from enterprise import saas_server
for r in saas_server.base_app.routes:
    if hasattr(r,'path') and ('analytics/events' in r.path or 'complete_onboarding' in r.path):
        print(sorted(r.methods), r.path)
"
# Expect:
#   ['POST'] /api/analytics/events/create-pr-button-clicked
#   ['POST'] /api/complete_onboarding
```

Manual testing completed:
Verified `create pr button clicked` and `onboarding completed` events fired in Posthog staging while on this branch.

Manual QA in staging after deploy:
1. Open a conversation, click the **Pull Request** button in the git control bar (and again from the **Tools → Create PR** submenu).
2. Complete the onboarding flow as a new user.
3. In PostHog, confirm `create pr button clicked` and `onboarding completed` events arrive (see "⚠️ event name compatibility" below if dashboards still look empty).

## Video/Screenshots

N/A — backend wiring + small frontend hook.

## Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Other

---

## ⚠️ Reviewer attention: event name compatibility

The emitted PostHog event strings follow the existing `analytics_constants.py` convention (lowercase with spaces):
- `'onboarding completed'` (not `onboarding_completed`)
- `'create pr button clicked'` (not `create_pr_button_clicked`)

This matches every other event migrated by #14006 (e.g. `'user logged in'`, `'conversation created'`). But the bug ticket references the **old client-side** names (snake_case). If PostHog dashboards / surveys / NPS triggers / cohorts are still configured against the old underscore names, they will keep showing "no events" after this PR is merged.

Two options — please pick one before merging:
- **A.** Keep the new spaces-convention names (this PR) and update PostHog-side configs (dashboards, the NPS survey trigger on the PR button, etc.) to match. Recommended for consistency with the rest of the analytics refactor.
- **B.** Override the constants to preserve the legacy snake_case names so dashboards don't have to change. Two-line change in `openhands/analytics/analytics_constants.py`. The same question applies to every other event migrated by #14006.

cc whoever owns the SaaS PostHog workspace.

---
